amendments.text: Index swap DDL must carry taken_down_at before created_at: CREATE INDEX idx_v0NN_published_recent ON planners (published, deleted_at, taken_down_at, created_at, category). The ratified shape omits taken_down_at, so the count query (WHERE taken_down_at IS NULL) can never be covering — spike-verified. Corrected shape: no-category count covering, both page variants filesort-free. DROP list unchanged. Residual decision: category-filtered count stays non-covering unless a second index (published, category, deleted_at, taken_down_at) is added — cost-based optimizer choice, cannot be promised by one ordered index.
amendments.enforcement: task-tier EXPLAIN probe re-run against migrated MySQL
amendments.resolution: user ratified as filed (5-tuple); second covering index for category count dismissed; manifest decision D19

amendments.text: Three coupled changes before phase 03: (1) replace the mechanics §5 INNER JOIN reconciliation with LEFT JOIN + IS NULL predicate (or pair it with a count-parity assertion) — the INNER JOIN reports false-clean when a planner lacks a stats row, and §5 is the cutover flip gate; (2) enforce the invariant every planner has a planner_stats row: create the stats row in the planner-creation transaction OR make dual-write increments INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE — mechanics §3 covers only the increment path; (3) pin the backfill to the unfiltered form (no WHERE published). Fixing only (1) leaves the gate dirty forever; fixing only (2) trusts upstream perfection. Happy-path backfill and dual-write mechanics spike-verified sound.
amendments.enforcement: stats-dual-write-consistent row + reconciliation checksum in SettingsBackfillMigrationIT-style migration test
amendments.resolution: user ratified with mechanism A only (creation-transaction insert, both PlannerCommandService sites) + unfiltered backfill; upsert hardening declined; manifest decision D20

amendments.text: D14 replaces the single SYNC_CONFLICT 409 code with a reason discriminator STALE_CLIENT|CONCURRENT_WRITE. CONCURRENT_WRITE is a new ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException handler in GlobalExceptionHandler (in scope). STALE_CLIENT requires PlannerConflictException to map to STALE_CLIENT instead of SYNC_CONFLICT. The existing PlannerControllerTest#updatePlanner_VersionMismatch_Returns409 (client version 999, ahead of server) asserts code=SYNC_CONFLICT and runs in the close-gate full suite; it is not in phase-01 scope. No in-scope-only change satisfies both the new stale row and that existing test. Proposed: add backend/src/test/java/org/danteplanner/backend/controller/PlannerControllerTest.java to phase-01 testExceptions so its one assertion updates SYNC_CONFLICT->STALE_CLIENT (serverVersion assertion unchanged).
amendments.enforcement: green-gate re-run with PlannerControllerTest in testExceptions; close-gate full suite green
amendments.resolution: User rejected the touch-scope expansion; deferred stale-client-409-reason. CONCURRENT_WRITE (oolfe row) implemented in-scope; SYNC_CONFLICT public code left intact.

amendments.text: The two ratified user-resolution rows drive userService.findOrCreateUser (the user-creation entry the tests call), but UserService.java was omitted from phase-02 touches (which lists UserSettingsService and auth/**). Add backend/src/main/java/org/danteplanner/backend/user/service/UserService.java to phase-02 touches so the resolution refactor (atomic user+settings creation; fresh-transaction retry on the uk_provider_provider_id duplicate-key race) can land. Touch-map completion for already-ratified rows, not a new feature.
amendments.enforcement: green-gate + close-gate over the two rows

amendments.origin: green-leg: flag-gated read rows need a runtime-mutable flag the test flips
amendments.text: The flag-on/flag-off read rows live in one test class (PlannerStatsCutoverIT) and must exercise both states, so the flag must be runtime-mutable (seeded from planner.stats.reads-enabled, flippable via setEnabled), matching the LineageRotationFlag exemplar. Add **/StatsReadsFlag.java to phase-03 stubAllow so the flag component (in planner/service, already in touches) can be created during the red window for the tests to reference.
amendments.enforcement: green-gate over the two flag rows

exceptions: deep set never ran at HEAD (collect --kick <HEAD>)
